method_atom: A construction that endows a lattice with a multi-parameter family of commutative, associative deformed products (a Frobenius-manifold structure: a flat pairing plus a potential whose third derivatives are structure constants obeying the WDVV associativity constraints), where the multiplication is encoded canonically by the combinatorial reflection data of a root system and is then extended from the symmetric to the general non-symmetric case. It is paired with a duality principle asserting that the deformed-product structure attached to a smooth desingularization of a combinatorial object can be predicted from, and matched to, the one attached to its singular/quotient presentation.
object_atom: The minimal (crepant) resolution Y of the DuVal/Kleinian singularity C^2/G, with G a finite subgroup of SU(2) of ADE type, and its orbifold counterpart [C^2/G]; concretely the C*-equivariant quantum cohomology ring of Y and the orbifold Gromov-Witten potential of the quotient.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the root-system-indexed family of WDVV/Frobenius associative products on a lattice plus the resolution-vs-quotient matching, while the ADE surface singularity, its resolution, and its quantum cohomology / Gromov-Witten invariants are the algebraic-geometry-specific object.
